steps.title: Spot the key phrase
steps.content: "On the basis of their instrumental value for promoting youth interests" is the pivot. Bidadanure is not claiming youth underrepresentation is inherently unfair — she accepts the consensus. Instead she argues on practical grounds: quotas would produce better outcomes for young people. That is an instrumental (outcome-based) argument, not a principled fairness one.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — "young adults may lack certain kinds of experience but those experiences are not relevant to the question of whether youth underrepresentation is fair in principle.": Contradicts the passage, which treats the experience concern as legitimate, not irrelevant. ✗
  • Choice B — "age-related quotas would alleviate the inherently unfair overrepresentation of older people that tends to occur in the world's democracies.": Claims underrepresentation is inherently unfair — directly contradicts the broad agreement the passage states. ✗
  • Choice C — "youth underrepresentation may be fair in principle but leads to worse outcomes for young adults than could be achieved with more proportionate youth representation.": Accepts that underrepresentation may be fair in principle (matching the consensus) yet argues quotas are justified by practical benefits — exactly an instrumental argument. ✓
  • Choice D — "it does not matter that youth underrepresentation is universal, temporary, and reflective of a legitimate concern since it is inherently unfair.": Again denies the passage's stated consensus that underrepresentation is not inherently unfair. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
